projectDescription: A Zig library that provides a high-level interface for interacting with NVIDIA GPUs via CUDA, enabling GPU memory management, kernel compilation and execution, and random number generation.
keyFeatures: Runtime CUDA kernel compilation from text or file via NVRTC
PTX module loading and kernel function execution
GPU random number generation via cuRAND
Automatic CUDA installation detection on Linux/macOS
Support for custom C/struct types on the GPU
Runtime and compile-time error handling with typed Zig errors
publicApiSurface: Device.default() — initialize GPU 0
Device.new(gpu: u16) — initialize specific GPU
Device.alloc(T, length) — allocate typed GPU memory
Device.allocZeros(T, length) — allocate zero-initialized GPU memory
Device.htodCopy(T, src) — copy slice from host to GPU
Device.htodCopyInto(T, src, dst) — copy into existing GPU slice
Device.dtohCopy(T, allocator, slice) — copy GPU slice to host
Device.syncReclaim(T, allocator, slice) — copy GPU slice to ArrayList
Device.loadPtx(file_path) — load PTX module from file
Device.loadPtxText(ptx) — load PTX module from text
Device.deinit() — release GPU context
CudaSlice(T).clone() — clone GPU buffer
CudaSlice(T).free() — free GPU buffer
Module.getFunc(name) — retrieve kernel function from module
Function.run(params, LaunchConfig) — launch CUDA kernel
LaunchConfig{ grid_dim, block_dim, shared_mem_bytes }
LaunchConfig.for_num_elems(n) — auto-configure for N elements
Compile.cudaText(src, options, allocator) — compile CUDA source to PTX
Compile.cudaFile(file, io, options, allocator) — compile CUDA file to PTX
Rng.default() — create RNG on GPU 0 with default seed
Rng.init(device, seed) — create RNG with specific device and seed
Rng.genrandom(size) — generate uniform random f32 values on GPU
CudaSliceR — runtime-typed GPU slice for dynamic dispatch
DType — enum of supported element types (f16, f32)
